steps.title: Identify the key requirement
steps.content: The conclusion states that anatomical features affect dogs' ability to distinguish speech from nonspeech. A supporting finding must demonstrate that dogs with different head shapes actually differ in this discrimination ability.

steps.title: Match to the answer that shows this
steps.content: Choice B demonstrates that shorter-headed dogs show a smaller difference in brain activity between speech (Spanish or Hungarian) and nonspeech (scrambled) compared to longer-headed dogs, directly proving that head shape affects speech-nonspeech discrimination. The other choices either compare within a single head shape (A), test language familiarity rather than speech-nonspeech (C), or confuse the comparison (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
